page: Microsoft Azure D32nls_v6 is a network-optimized, low-memory virtual machine belonging to the Dnlv6 server family. Powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features an x86_64 architecture with a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor. The CPU configuration delivers 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. It includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix operations.

The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This low-memory profile represents a design tradeoff, prioritizing compute density and network performance over large in-memory capacities. There is no local storage bundled with this server, though it is premium storage capable, requiring external block storage attachment. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

In benchmark evaluations, the D32nls_v6 demonstrates strong performance in large-block read-only memory bandwidth (202.93 GB/sec), multi-threaded bzip3 decompression (175.85 GB/sec), and static web serving throughput (46.21 GB/sec), all placing in the top 25% tier. Other metrics, including stress-ng CPU performance, Redis SET operations, and Passmark database operations, fall within the average range. This profile makes the instance highly suitable for network-intensive workloads, compute-heavy tasks with low memory footprints, static web serving, and multi-threaded decompression tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure D32nls_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the network-optimized, low-memory Dnlv6 family. Running on Microsoft Hyper-V, it features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and a 4.2 GHz clock speed. The hardware includes 64.0 GB of RAM, representing a low 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio, and lacks local storage, though it supports premium external storage. Performance benchmarks show strong results in large-block read-only memory bandwidth (202.93 GB/sec), multi-threaded bzip3 decompression (175.85 GB/sec), and static web serving throughput (46.21 GB/sec), while CPU and database operations perform in the average range. This configuration is optimized for network-heavy workloads, static web serving, and compute tasks that do not require high memory density.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32nls_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong performance in static web serving and large-block memory reads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32nls_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a dedicated 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A network-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated high-frequency processors and a low memory-to-core ratio for compute-dense workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz
32 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per vCPU)
Strong performance in static web serving and multi-threaded decompression
No bundled local storage, with support for premium external storage
Equipped with AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX)
Best suited for network-intensive applications and compute-heavy tasks
